commands.review_summary:
The PR makes exact-review per-target concurrency configurable, sets production to four same-target slots under the existing global cap, updates wake scheduling and stats, documents the limit, and adds queue tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main hard-codes one active exact-review item per target and skips pending same-target items even when global slots remain. I did not run the PR's verification commands because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human merge judgment is needed because this maintainer-authored PR changes production exact-review fanout and has no narrow repair finding for automation to address.

target.title: fix(queue): use available exact-review capacity per target
target.body:
## Summary

- Make exact-review concurrency per target configurable and set production to use the existing global four-slot capacity.
- Keep the global cap unchanged while allowing the OpenClaw backlog to drain instead of leaving three slots idle.
- Update alarm wake scheduling so pending same-target items are retried while target capacity remains available.

## Verification

- node --test test/dashboard-worker.test.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.dashboard.json
- node scripts/check-limits.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check dashboard/worker.ts dashboard/wrangler.toml docs/limits.md test/dashboard-worker.test.ts
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Operational note: this does not raise the global exact-review queue cap; it only stops the one-per-target guard from starving the existing four slots.
target.author: vincentkoc
